name: Tierfs Package Coverage

tasks.title: Cover Resolve, ResolveAll, and WriteBase error paths
tasks.description: Cover Resolve, ResolveAll, and WriteBase error paths
tasks.body:
Three methods have uncovered error branches:

Resolve (87.5%, line 53): The uncovered path is line 61, where os.ReadFile returns an error that is NOT os.ErrNotExist (e.g. permission denied). To test: create a tier directory with a file that has 000 permissions, then call Resolve for that file. Verify the returned error wraps the permission error with the "tierfs: resolve X in tier Y" message.

ResolveAll (87.5%, line 69): Two possible uncovered paths:
1. Line 79: os.ReadDir returns a non-ErrNotExist error. Test by making a tier subdirectory unreadable (000 permissions).
2. Line 87: os.ReadFile fails for an individual .md file. Test by creating a .md file with 000 permissions inside a readable directory.

WriteBase (66.7%, line 97): Two error paths:
1. Line 100: os.MkdirAll fails. Test by making the base tier directory read-only so subdirectory creation fails.
2. Line 103: os.WriteFile fails. Test by making the target directory read-only after MkdirAll succeeds (or write to a path where the parent exists but is read-only).

Note: Permission-based tests should check runtime.GOOS != "windows" and skip on Windows, and should also skip if running as root (os.Getuid() == 0) since root ignores permission bits.

Target: raise internal/tierfs from 86.1% to 94%+.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Tierfs Package Coverage is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify Resolve non-ErrNotExist path, ResolveAll ReadDir/ReadFile error paths, and WriteBase MkdirAll/WriteFile error paths are all tested. Confirm coverage >= 94%.

breadcrumbs.text: Added permission-based error path tests for Resolve (line 61), ResolveAll (lines 79, 87), and WriteBase (lines 100, 103). Coverage raised from 86.1% to 100%.

Audit PASS: build clean, all 10 tests pass with race detector, 100% statement coverage, go vet and gofmt clean. Code review found no nil safety, error handling, concurrency, or quality issues. ADRs and spec exist and are substantive.

audit.result_summary: Raised internal/tierfs test coverage from 86.1% to 100% by adding permission-based error path tests for Resolve (non-ErrNotExist from ReadFile), ResolveAll (ReadDir permission error and ReadFile permission error), and WriteBase (MkdirAll failure and WriteFile failure). All tests use t.TempDir with platform-aware skip guards for permission tests. The package builds, vets, and formats clean with no race conditions detected.
